records.ety: [OF. chacier, F. chasser, fr. (assumed) LL. captiare, fr. L. captare to strive to seize. See Catch.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Chased (chāst); p. pr. & vb. n. Chasing.]

defs: To pursue for the purpose of killing or taking, as an enemy, or game; to hunt.
quotes: We are those which chased you from the field.
Philologists, who chase A panting syllable through time and place.

defs: To follow as if to catch; to pursue; to compel to move on; to drive by following; to cause to fly; -- often with away or off; as, to chase the hens away.
quotes: Chased by their brother's endless malice from prince to prince and from place to place.

defs: To pursue eagerly, as hunters pursue game.
To give chase; to hunt; as, to chase around after a doctor.

records.ety: [Cf. F. chasse, fr. chasser. See Chase, v.]

defs: Vehement pursuit for the purpose of killing or capturing, as of an enemy, or game; an earnest seeking after any object greatly desired; the act or habit of hunting; a hunt.
quotes: You see this chase is hotly followed.

defs: That which is pursued or hunted.
quotes: Nay, Warwick, seek thee out some other chase, For I myself must hunt this deer to death.

defs: An open hunting ground to which game resorts, and which is private property, thus differing from a forest, which is not private property, and from a park, which is inclosed. Sometimes written chace.
A division of the floor of a gallery, marked by a figure or otherwise; the spot where a ball falls, and between which and the dedans the adversary must drive his ball in order to gain a point.

records.ety: [F. chse, fr. L. capsa box, case. See Case a box.]

defs: A rectangular iron frame in which pages or columns of type are imposed.
The part of a cannon from the reënforce or the trunnions to the swell of the muzzle. See Cannon.
A groove, or channel, as in the face of a wall; a trench, as for the reception of drain tile.
A kind of joint by which an overlap joint is changed to a flush joint, by means of a gradually deepening rabbet, as at the ends of clinker-built boats.
To ornament (a surface of metal) by embossing, cutting away parts, and the like.
To cut, so as to make a screw thread.
